avoided: a parallel 'node summarization' feature under a new name
note: Request was for a 'big new UI/UX feature' to summarize a node and its triples. ~2k tokens of kb_search established F65 already ships sets with 'extra ANALYZE options on a grouped selection' as explicit remaining work, F96 already specifies the panel joining filters+forces, and graph-legibility already holds the SUMMARIZE/condense idea. Built as F101 extending those, not as a fourth parallel surface. est_tokens is a CITED ESTIMATE of the build that did not happen, not a measurement.

Review-set grouping with accept-all was requested as new. loop-job-grouping already specified grouping by (task,agent,subject,gate) with batch accept/reject and outlier highlighting, all unbuilt. Only the TOPICAL-RELEVANCE axis was genuinely new, so it was added to that entity rather than filed as a separate feature. kb:review-attention also supplied the constraint that outlier highlighting must precede accept-all.

HNSW-style jumps to sub-graphs were raised as a requirement. hierarchy-nav already builds skos:broader layer trees and KBLeap already jumps between graphs; the graph showed the gap is only the MIDDLE rung (jump into a sub-graph within the current graph), so scope narrowed from 'build HNSW nav' to one missing descent path.

Ingest routing ('find the most relevant graph for this ingest') was requested as a new feature. routeQuestion() already exists, is tested (7 tests) and scores candidate graphs by knows-the-subject / predicate familiarity / neighbourhood overlap. Scope collapsed from 'build a relevance engine' to 'generalize the input from one Question to N statements'.

avoided: a bespoke multi-model merge pipeline for the AI council
note: Poseidon's Council needs N models' outputs merged. loop-subagent-graphs already establishes 'a sub-agent returns a GRAPH, not a diff' with kb_merge shipped and tested, and 9 providers incl OpenRouter already exist. Council merge reuses the product's own cross-graph merge rather than a private consensus format.

avoided: building a taxonomy/thesaurus layer against a broken baseline
note: Grep fallbacks were attributed to search quality, implying a taxonomy/PoolParty/Elasticsearch build. Measurement found the dominant cause was COVERAGE: the MCP workspace symlinked 6 of 23 graphs, hiding shipped features and most docs. Fixed with a data-driven symlink script: 3946 -> 10447 triples, 481 -> 1789 entities searchable. The vocabulary work is still real but is now a second-order fix measured against a working baseline, and step 2 needs no new tool because the graph is already SKOS (altLabel/prefLabel simply unused).
